questions.q: Who was consecrated emperor at the Rajasuya yajna?
questions.explanation: The Rajasuya crowned Yudhishthira as samrat, emperor over the kings of the earth.

questions.q: In which city was the Rajasuya sacrifice performed?
questions.explanation: Yudhishthira held the great sacrifice in his own capital of Indraprastha.

questions.q: What does performing the Rajasuya proclaim about a king?
options: His mastery of the four Vedas
His conquest of old age and death
His supremacy as emperor over other kings
questions.explanation: The Rajasuya is the imperial consecration, announcing that all other kings accept the sacrificer's overlordship.

questions.q: Who was chosen to receive the agra-puja, the first honor of the assembly?
questions.explanation: The first worship was offered to Krishna, judged the most worthy guest among all the assembled kings and sages.

questions.q: Which king disrupted the ceremony with a stream of insults against Krishna?
questions.explanation: Shishupala raged that a cowherd had been honored above kings and hurled abuse at Krishna before the court.

questions.q: With what weapon did Krishna finally silence Shishupala?
questions.explanation: When the insults passed the hundredth, Krishna loosed the Sudarshana discus and beheaded Shishupala.

questions.q: How many of Shishupala's offenses had Krishna promised to forgive?
questions.explanation: Krishna had vowed to pardon a hundred offenses, and the hundred-and-first sealed Shishupala's fate.

questions.q: Whose death cleared the way for Yudhishthira to attempt the Rajasuya?
questions.explanation: Krishna counseled that no Rajasuya was possible while Jarasandha of Magadha lived and held kings captive.

questions.q: Which kingdom did Shishupala rule?
questions.explanation: Shishupala was the king of Chedi, which is why he is often called the Chedi king or Chaidya.

questions.q: Which prince left the Rajasuya consumed by envy of the Pandavas' splendor?
questions.explanation: Duryodhana went home sick with envy, and his brooding led straight to the game of dice.

questions.q: Which Pandava led the northern campaign of conquest before the yajna?
questions.explanation: In the digvijaya, Arjuna marched north while his brothers subdued the other three directions.

questions.q: Which Pandava marched east to subdue kings for the sacrifice?
questions.explanation: Bhima conquered the eastern kingdoms, bringing their tribute back for the Rajasuya.

questions.q: Who recommended that Krishna deserved the first honor of the assembly?
questions.explanation: When Yudhishthira asked who should be honored first, Bhishma named Krishna without hesitation.

questions.q: Which Pandava ritually offered the arghya, the first worship, to Krishna?
questions.explanation: At Bhishma's word, Sahadeva presented the arghya to Krishna before the assembled kings.

questions.q: Which celestial sage brought word of Pandu's wish that his son perform the Rajasuya?
questions.explanation: Narada visited Indraprastha and conveyed that Pandu, watching from the heavens, longed to see his son consecrated emperor.

questions.q: What family tie bound Krishna to Shishupala?
options: Shishupala was the son of Krishna's father's sister
They had shared the same guru, Sandipani
questions.explanation: Shishupala was Krishna's cousin, born to his paternal aunt Shrutashravas, queen of Chedi.

questions.q: To whom had Krishna promised that he would pardon a hundred offenses?
options: To the sage Narada in Dwarka
To Shishupala's mother, his own aunt
questions.explanation: Krishna gave his frightened aunt Shrutashravas his word that he would forgive her son a hundred times.

questions.q: What marked Shishupala's body at his birth?
options: Golden armor fused to his skin
questions.explanation: Shishupala was born with an extra eye and two extra arms, which vanished when Krishna took him on his lap, marking Krishna as his destined slayer.

questions.q: What did the onlookers see when Shishupala fell dead?
options: His body turned instantly to ash
A flock of crows darkened the hall
A light left his body and merged into Krishna
His crown rolled to Yudhishthira's feet
questions.explanation: A blazing energy rose from the slain king and entered Krishna, showing his soul absorbed into the divine.

questions.q: What duty did Duryodhana perform at the Rajasuya?
options: Receiving the tribute brought by the kings
questions.explanation: Duryodhana was made receiver of tributes, and handling that river of wealth fed his envy.

questions.q: Which humble task did Krishna take upon himself during the sacrifice?
options: Washing the feet of the brahmins
questions.explanation: Krishna chose to wash the feet of the brahmins, and some retellings add that he cleared away the guests' used leaf plates.

questions.q: What humiliation did Duryodhana suffer at Indraprastha after the yajna?
options: He was denied a seat beside Bhishma
He fell into a pool he mistook for a crystal floor
He lost a debate to Vidura before the kings
questions.explanation: In Maya's hall of illusions Duryodhana mistook water for crystal and fell in, and the laughter that followed festered into hatred.

questions.q: From which southern ruler did Sahadeva's campaign collect tribute through envoys?
questions.explanation: Sahadeva's southern march reached so far that envoys brought back tribute of jewels from Vibhishana of Lanka.

questions.q: What warning did Vyasa give Yudhishthira at the close of the Rajasuya?
options: That Draupadi would bring ruin upon the Kurus
That portents foretold the destruction of the kshatriyas with him as the occasion
That Krishna would abandon the Pandavas in war
That Indraprastha would sink beneath the Yamuna
questions.explanation: Shaken by Vyasa's prophecy, Yudhishthira vowed never to refuse or speak harshly to his elders, a vow that later bound him to accept the dice summons.

questions.q: In whose service had Shishupala risen as supreme commander before the Rajasuya?
questions.explanation: Shishupala had served as commander of Jarasandha's forces, which deepened his hatred of Krishna.

questions.q: As which earlier beings had Shishupala lived, according to the doorkeepers-of-Vishnu tradition?
questions.explanation: Puranic tradition attached to the epic makes Shishupala the third birth of Vishnu's cursed gatekeeper Jaya, after Hiranyakashipu and Ravana.

questions.q: Which duty at the yajna was entrusted to Vidura?
questions.explanation: Wise Vidura was placed in charge of the treasury and expenses while other Kurus handled food, gifts, and tribute.

questions.q: Nakula's western march secured the allegiance of which kinsman king?
questions.explanation: Conquering the west, Nakula received the willing submission of his maternal uncle Shalya, king of Madra.

questions.q: Who was set upon the Chedi throne after Shishupala's death?
questions.explanation: Shishupala's son Dhrishtaketu was installed as king of Chedi and later fought for the Pandavas at Kurukshetra.

questions.q: Which sage officiated as the chief brahma priest of the Rajasuya?
questions.explanation: Vyasa himself served as the brahma priest, with sages such as Yajnavalkya and Paila in the other priestly roles.
